name: Adaptive Reinforcement Learning for Unobservable Random Delays

description: arXiv:2506.14411v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: In standard reinforcement learning (RL) settings, the interaction between the agent and the environment is typically modeled as a Markov decision process (MDP), which assumes that the agent observes the system state instantaneously, selects an action without delay, and executes it immediately. author: John Wikman, Alexandre Proutiere, David Broman
